prompt: What does membersihkan mean?
opt_a_why: Correct - bersih plus -kan makes the causative make clean.
opt_b_why: That is just the adjective bersih.
opt_d_why: The -kan suffix does not make a doer noun.
explanation: -kan often makes a causative verb: membersihkan means to make something clean.

prompt: One main job of the -kan suffix is to make a verb...
opt_a_why: Correct - -kan can add a make-it-happen meaning.
opt_b_why: Malay verbs have no tense.
explanation: -kan makes verbs causative (make something) or benefactive (do it for someone).

prompt: Which verb means to explain (to make clear), from the root jelas?
opt_a_why: Correct - jelas plus me- and -kan.
opt_b_why: The ber- prefix does not fit here.
opt_d_why: The -i suffix would point to a place.
explanation: jelas plus -kan gives menjelaskan, to make clear.

prompt: Make the verb to buy for someone, from the root beli
explanation: beli plus me- and -kan gives membelikan - to buy for someone.

prompt: The -i suffix usually signals that the object is...
opt_a_why: Correct - -i often points to a location.
opt_b_why: It is not limited to people.
explanation: -i tends to mark a place or surface, as in menduduki (to occupy).

prompt: Which verb means to visit someone, from the root kunjung?
opt_a_why: Correct - kunjung plus me- and -i.
opt_b_why: The -kan suffix would change the meaning.
opt_c_why: berkunjung is intransitive and cannot take an object directly.
explanation: mengunjungi means to visit a person or place.

prompt: From the root masuk, which verb means to enter a place?
opt_a_why: Correct - memasuki acts on the place you enter.
opt_b_why: memasukkan means to put something in.
opt_c_why: This is not a real verb.
explanation: memasuki means to enter; memasukkan means to put something in.

prompt: The rule of thumb is: -kan moves something, and -i...
opt_a_why: Correct - -i typically acts on a location or surface.
opt_b_why: Neither suffix forms questions.
explanation: -kan moves an object; -i acts on a place.

prompt: The verb terjatuh suggests the fall was...
opt_a_why: Correct - ter- here means it happened unintentionally.
opt_b_why: ter- signals the opposite of planned.
explanation: ter- can mark an accidental action: terjatuh means fell by accident.

prompt: What does terbesar mean?
opt_a_why: Correct - ter- on an adjective makes a superlative.
opt_b_why: That would be an accidental ter- verb.
opt_c_why: ter- does not mark the future.
explanation: ter- plus an adjective means the most: terbesar is biggest.

prompt: Which is NOT a job of the ter- prefix?
opt_a_why: Correct - ter- never marks tense; Malay has no tenses.
opt_b_why: ter- does do this, as in terlupa.
opt_c_why: ter- does do this, as in terbaik.
opt_d_why: ter- does do this, as in tidak terangkat.
explanation: ter- marks accidental actions, superlatives and ability - but never tense.

prompt: Make the accidental verb meaning I forgot, using ter- with the root lupa
explanation: lupa plus ter- gives terlupa - forgot accidentally.

prompt: What does sudah mean?
opt_a_why: Correct - sudah marks a completed action.
opt_b_why: Not yet is belum.
explanation: sudah marks something already done.

prompt: What does belum mean?
opt_a_why: Correct - belum means not yet.
opt_b_why: Already is sudah.
explanation: belum means the action has not happened yet.

prompt: What does Saya sedang makan mean?
opt_a_why: Correct - sedang marks an action in progress.
opt_b_why: That would use sudah.
explanation: sedang marks an action in progress, like English -ing.

prompt: The marker akan shows that an action is...
opt_a_why: Correct - akan means will or going to.
opt_b_why: A completed past uses sudah or telah.
explanation: akan marks the future: Saya akan pergi means I will go.

prompt: What does pernah mean?
opt_a_why: Correct - pernah marks past experience.
opt_b_why: Never is tidak pernah.
explanation: pernah marks experience: Saya pernah ke Melaka means I have been to Melaka.

prompt: What does kerana mean?
opt_a_why: Correct - kerana gives a reason.
opt_b_why: But is tetapi.
explanation: kerana (or sebab) means because.

prompt: What does walaupun mean?
opt_a_why: Correct - walaupun concedes a point.
opt_b_why: Because is kerana.
explanation: walaupun (or meskipun) means although.

prompt: Which word means so that?
opt_a_why: Correct - supaya (or agar) means so that.
opt_b_why: kerana means because.
explanation: supaya and agar both mean so that, giving a purpose.

prompt: Which word means but?
opt_a_why: Correct - tetapi (spoken: tapi) means but.
opt_b_why: dan means and.
explanation: tetapi means but; the colloquial form is tapi.

prompt: In orang yang tinggi, the word yang links the noun to...
opt_a_why: Correct - yang introduces a description of the noun.
opt_b_why: yang does not mark number.
opt_d_why: yang is not a location word.
explanation: yang links a noun to its description: orang yang tinggi means the tall person.

prompt: How do you say the book that I bought?
opt_a_why: Correct - noun, then yang, then the description.
opt_b_why: yang must come right after the noun buku.
explanation: The pattern is noun plus yang plus description: buku yang saya beli.

prompt: What does yang merah mean?
opt_a_why: Correct - yang plus an adjective means the ... one.
opt_b_why: Very red would be sangat merah.
opt_c_why: Not red would be tidak merah.
explanation: yang plus an adjective picks out the ... one: yang merah means the red one.

prompt: Translate to Malay: the person who is tall
explanation: orang (person) plus yang plus tinggi (tall).
